name: Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop from Alcaraz
name_original: Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Alcaraz
description: Cycle the difficult 44.7-mile Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop, exploring tunnels, viaducts, and diverse landscapes near Alcaraz.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcaraz traverse a mountainous region in southeast Spain, characterized by diverse landscapes. The area features a blend of wide plains, rugged foothills, and historical infrastructure like viaducts and tunnels. Limestone formations, pine and oak forests, and ravines define much of the terrain, offering varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Alcaraz The most popular touring cycling route is Riópar Viejo por Batán del Puerto — circular desde Alcaraz, a 55.5 miles (89.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Paterna del Madera — circular desde Alcaraz, a difficult 41.9 miles (67.5 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and descents through the region's natural environment. Local touring cyclists also love the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop from Alcaraz, a 44.7 miles (72.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain including sections of the Vía Verde, often completed in about 4 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcaraz is defined by mountainous terrain, historical railway infrastructure, and diverse forests and ravines. The network offers options for challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Alcaraz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcaraz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcaraz?
A: There are 14 touring cycling routes available around Alcaraz. These routes offer a variety of experiences through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Alcaraz?
A: The terrain around Alcaraz is quite varied. You'll encounter wide plains with agricultural landscapes, especially towards La Mancha, transitioning to more rugged, mountainous scenery as you approach the Sierra. Routes often feature compacted earth and tarmac surfaces, gravel paths, and forest trails. The region is characterized by limestone formations, pine and oak forests, and ravines, providing diverse cycling environments.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Alcaraz?
A: Yes, several circular touring cycling routes start from Alcaraz. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357660930/riopar-viejo-por-batan-del-puerto-circular-desde-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo por Batán del Puerto — circular desde Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 89.4 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357302928/paterna-del-madera-circular-desde-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paterna del Madera — circular desde Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 67.5 km path with significant climbs and descents.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while cycling around Alcaraz?
A: The Alcaraz region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, known for its spectacular waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views. You might also encounter charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; itself, with its historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5919080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, or the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcaraz?
A: The best times to visit Alcaraz for touring cycling are spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant, making for ideal cycling conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly in the mountainous areas.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz is a significant feature for touring cyclists. You can explore sections of it on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063172401/sierra-de-alcaraz-greenway-loop-from-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop from Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;. This greenway follows an abandoned railway line, offering a unique journey through tunnels, trenches, and viaducts. The final 24 kilometers towards Alcaraz are particularly scenic.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Alcaraz?
A: Most touring cycling routes in Alcaraz are rated as difficult, with significant elevation gains and challenging climbs. For instance, 13 out of the 14 available routes are classified as difficult. While the Vía Verde sections generally have a gentle gradient, some moderate climbs exist, especially towards the end of the greenway. Cyclists should be prepared for varied and often demanding terrain.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Alcaraz?
A: Yes, the mountainous nature of the Sierra de Alcaraz means many routes offer spectacular natural viewpoints. The region is also recognized as a Starlight Destination, providing unparalleled views of the night sky from designated spots. You can expect panoramic vistas of the surrounding countryside, especially as you gain elevation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcaraz?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcaraz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from wide plains to rugged mountains, and the unique experience of cycling along historical railway infrastructure like the Vía Verde. The challenging nature of many routes and the opportunity to explore less crowded natural areas are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find routes that include lakes or lagoons in the Alcaraz area?
A: Yes, the region features several beautiful lagoons. You can find routes that pass by or near natural reserves such as the Laguna Ojo del Arquillo. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357334108/monumento-natural-laguna-del-arquillo-from-the-santuario-virgen-de-cortes-and-return-via-the-via-verde-de-la-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural Laguna del Arquillo from the Santuario Virgen de Cortes and return via the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to experience this natural monument.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the historical infrastructure of the region?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz is a prime example, transforming an unfinished railway line into a cycling path. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357374499/los-batanes-salobre-and-estrecho-del-hocino-loop-from-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Batanes, Salobre and Estrecho del Hocino Loop from Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; may also incorporate elements of the region's historical and natural features, offering a blend of cultural and scenic exploration.
tips: Shortly after Alcaraz, the Via Verde begins towards Albacete, which is about 75 km long. A real highlight!

There are several viaducts that you will find on the greenway, but this one in particular stands out for the radical change of landscape when crossing it. You enter a more rugged and colorful terrain with the mountains and Alcaraz in the background.
Great bridges and tunnels on the Via Verde de la Alcaraz.
One of the most beautiful Via Verdes 👍
The Alcaraz greenway has 25 tunnels distributed along its route. The longest is 750 meters. Some of them are not illuminated. For safety reasons, the use of lighting inside the tunnels is mandatory.

The old Robledo station has been recovered as rural accommodation. It is the best option to spend the night without deviating from the greenway.

Just one kilometer away is the town of Robledo where there are bars, grocery stores and a pharmacy.

If your objective is simply to make a stop before continuing on your way, you can find a rest area with shade, drinking water and information signs a few meters from the road.
One of the rest areas of the Ruta del Quijote. It is very close to the town of El Jardín, where supplies can be obtained if necessary.

Without a doubt, the Sanctuary of Cortes is the most visited place in the province of Albacete.
In 1976, an article by W. A. Christian on Spanish sanctuaries had been published; in it he made a classification of the importance of these centers of popular devotion, today also called local, based on the geographical area occupied by the devotees who went to each one of them; established four national sanctuaries and a dozen regional ones; Among the latter was that of the Virgin of Cortes de Alcaraz, this being the only one in the Castilian-La Mancha region that was considered to be of this category. From this, without a doubt, it was inferred that the Cortes was a very significant sanctuary, and today it maintains that character.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally in spring (for wildflowers like poppies) or autumn, when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially in the more exposed sections, so if you go then, start early to avoid the midday heat. Winter can be pleasant but might be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop from Alcaraz?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of Alcaraz itself, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for this loop. Specific parking areas for the greenway might be available near the access points to the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz is generally dog-friendly. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially in sections where you might encounter other cyclists, hikers, or wildlife. Remember to bring enough water for your dog, as water sources can be scarce.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop. It's a public greenway, freely accessible to cyclists and hikers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop, and what bike is recommended?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, primarily consisting of compacted earth and gravel, with some asphalted sections. As you approach the Sierra de Alcaraz, it becomes more rugged and mountainous. A mountain bike or a hybrid bike with mixed-tread tires is highly recommended to handle the diverse surfaces comfortably.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The full loop is approximately 72 km (45 miles) long. For a touring cyclist, it typically takes around 4 hours and 46 minutes of moving time to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or points of interest.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable points, including the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz itself, impressive railway heritage like the Viaduct to Alcaraz and beautiful old bridges, and tunnels such as the Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel. You'll also pass by Robledo Station, rest areas, and the significant Sanctuary of Our Lady of Cortes.

faqs.question: Are there water sources or amenities available along the route?
faqs.answer: While there are some facilities like Robledo Station and rest areas, sections of the route may lack water sources and services. It's crucial to carry sufficient water, snacks, and any necessary supplies, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: Are there any tunnels on the route, and do I need special equipment for them?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes numerous tunnels, such as the Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel. Some of these tunnels are unlit, so it is essential to carry a torch or a cycle lamp for visibility and safety.

faqs.question: Does the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz for a significant portion. It also intersects with sections of the Sendero de los Serranos and other local paths like Camino historico Paterna - Alcaraz and Camino acceso a Vía Verde.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: As a greenway, it generally features gentle slopes, making it accessible. The initial parts are flatter, but the route gradually becomes more challenging with moderate climbs, especially towards the Sierra de Alcaraz. While suitable for many, some sections might require good fitness, and beginners should be prepared for varying surfaces and some uphill efforts.
